name: github-release-management
description: Comprehensive GitHub release orchestration with AI swarm coordination for automated versioning, testing, deployment, and rollback management
capabilities: Orchestrates multi-agent release workflows with 6 specialized AI agents
Automates semantic versioning based on commit analysis and breaking changes
Coordinates cross-platform builds for npm, Docker, and binary artifacts
Implements progressive deployment with automated rollback triggers
Generates categorized changelogs with migration guides
Monitors release health with real-time metrics and alerts
author: Claude Flow Team
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill providing GitHub release management examples. All 150 static findings are false positives - shell command examples in documentation, standard GitHub Actions credential usage, and legitimate CI/CD patterns. No actual executable code with vulnerabilities exists. Safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: Managing software releases involves multiple complex steps that often lead to errors and delays. This skill orchestrates your entire release pipeline using AI agents that handle versioning, testing, deployment, and monitoring automatically.
userTitle: Automate GitHub Releases with AI Swarm Coordination
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI and proper repository permissions
Advanced features need claude-flow and swarm coordination setup
Performance depends on project size and test suite complexity

useCases.title: Automated Release Pipeline
useCases.description: Set up complete CI/CD release workflows that handle versioning, testing, and deployment across multiple platforms automatically.

useCases.title: Multi-Package Coordination
useCases.description: Coordinate releases across multiple repositories with dependency validation and synchronized versioning.

useCases.title: Staged Production Rollouts
useCases.description: Implement canary deployments with automated health monitoring and instant rollback capabilities for critical systems.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a patch release v1.2.4 with automated changelog generation and npm publishing

promptTemplates.title: Feature Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan and execute v2.1.0 release with breaking change detection, multi-platform builds, and staged deployment

promptTemplates.title: Emergency Hotfix
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy emergency hotfix v1.5.1 for security vulnerability with fast-track testing and immediate rollout

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Repo Coordination
promptTemplates.prompt: Coordinate simultaneous release of frontend v3.0.0, backend v2.5.0, and API v1.8.0 with compatibility validation

bestPractices: Always validate and sanitize version strings, branch names, and user inputs before using in commands
Use allowlists for deployment targets and limit file system access to specific directories
Implement proper credential management with secure token storage and rotation
antiPatterns: Never interpolate user input directly into shell commands without validation
Avoid using dynamic file paths without path traversal protection
Do not expose credentials in command logs or process output

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with private GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with both public and private repositories when authenticated with appropriate GitHub tokens.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for GitHub API calls?
faq.answer: The skill respects GitHub rate limits of 5000 requests per hour for authenticated users with automatic retry.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my existing CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides GitHub Actions workflows and integrates with Jenkins, GitLab CI, and other systems.

faq.question: How does the skill handle rollback on failed deployments?
faq.answer: The skill includes automated rollback based on error rates, latency thresholds, and availability metrics.

faq.question: What platforms can this skill deploy to?
faq.answer: Supports npm, Docker Hub, GitHub Packages, S3, and custom deployment targets with extensible configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill require any specific tools to be installed?
faq.answer: GitHub CLI (gh) is required. Optional tools include claude-flow, ruv-swarm, and mcp-github for enhanced features.
